hypothesis: If AMDEN, implemented with an EGNN backbone and VE/VP SDE noise schedules (using ghost atoms for density control) and trained on MD-generated amorphous datasets, then it will generate structures whose RDFs, bond-angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions closely match those of the corresponding MD reference datasets across different thermal histories and compositions, with the energy-based denoiser plus HMC refinement producing significantly better agreement after local geometry optimization than standard Euler–Maruyama denoising, and classifier-free guidance conditioned on Young’s modulus enabling generated MEG samples to achieve the targeted mechanical properties.
